extra.message: Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into exec, where it is executed as Python code. This may result in a Code Injection vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into exec, where it is executed as Python code. This may result in a Code Injection vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into access of the globals() dictionary, where it is executed as Python code. This may result in a Code Injection vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into open, where it is used as a path. This may result in a Path Traversal vulnerability and allow an attacker to read arbitrary files.
Avoid hardcoding values that are meant to be secret. Found a hardcoded string used in here.
CSRF protection is disabled by django.views.decorators.csrf.csrf_exempt. This allows the attackers to execute requests on a user's behalf.
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into django.shortcuts.redirect, where it is used as input for request redirection. This may result in an Open Redirect vulnerability.

Unsanitized input from data from a remote resource flows into html, where it is used to dynamically construct the HTML page on client side. This may result in a DOM Based Cross-Site Scripting attack (DOMXSS).
Cookie's HttpOnly flag is set to False by default. Set it to true to protect the cookie from possible malicious code on client side.
Cookie's Secure flag is set to False by default. Set it to true to protect the cookie from man-in-the-middle attacks.
